data.executiveSummary: The frontend-web service experienced significant latency degradation in the last 30 minutes due to an N+1 query issue identified in the database operations, particularly with the 'SELECT * FROM users_cart' query.

rootCause.title: N+1 Query Issue
rootCause.description: An N+1 query issue was identified where multiple sequential 'SELECT * FROM users_cart' database queries were executed within single request traces. This pattern significantly increased the latency of requests.

evidenceChain: Multiple sequential 'SELECT * FROM users_cart' database queries appearing in single request traces.
High p95 latency detected with GET /api/checkout, consistent with N+1 query problem.

timeline.event: Trace LVRQpiMj7zdppK7P8koxcQ== shows multiple sequential 'SELECT * FROM users_cart' queries.
High CPU usage at 92.07%, but no direct correlation with request latency.

data.impact: Users experienced significant response time delays when accessing the checkout functionality of the frontend-web service.
recommendedActions: Implement eager loading to address the N+1 query issue for 'users_cart' relations to reduce the number of database queries and improve performance.
